reason: Closes #996: fixes when a queued waypoint leg's own stance (order_mode) takes effect -- previously the leg's recorded mode was captured but never applied to the unit, so it silently inherited whatever stance was already active; now it applies exactly when the queue promotes that leg to current. This is a pure internal-state timing fix with no live visual indicator: checked existing debug overlays first (per the 'check for existing debug-visual precedent' convention) -- HUD's amber 'Order: X' label (HUD.set_order_mode) reflects the ARMED mode at the moment a stance key is pressed, not a selected unit's live order_mode; the recorded-order click-pulse overlay (SelectionManager's demo-order playback draw) likewise tags each click with the armed mode AT THAT CLICK, not whether/when it was later applied to the unit. Neither would visibly differ before vs. after this fix. Verified instead via GUT tests reading Unit.order_mode directly across the append -> promote sequence (test_appended_leg_applies_its_own_stance_once_promoted, test_append_to_idle_unit_applies_its_own_armed_mode).
